steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures of each choice
steps.content:
The incorrect choices present a noun plus a finite verb: “authors asserted,” “authors were asserting,” or “authors, had asserted.”

The correct choice presents a noun plus a comma and participle: “authors, asserting.” Since the main verb helped appears later in the sentence, the blank must not introduce another finite verb that would create structural ambiguity.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for participial modifiers
steps.content:
A comma separates a noun from a participial phrase modifying it.

The phrase authors, asserting that American publishers' appropriation of their work caused, in the words of the petition, “deep and extensive injuries...on their reputation and property,” uses a participle to modify the noun while helped remains the sole main verb.

A comma before a finite verb does not follow the participial modifier rule, and the other finite-verb choices create structural ambiguity. D is correct.
